activities: Free kids' bounce house (4–8 PM, ages 2–14)

id_requirements.dod_cardholders: Enter through Mapes/32, Mapes/175, or Rockenbach/175 gates
id_requirements.non_military: Enter through Reece/175 gate with REAL ID-compliant driver's license, ID card, or U.S. passport. All passengers 18+ need valid ID.

what_to_bring: REAL ID-compliant license, ID, or passport for every adult

itinerary.activity: Leave home, head to the correct gate for your ID type
Event opens. Bounce house, food trucks/vendors, and DJ are live
Dinner from vendors, walk the field, claim a viewing spot
Hottest stretch of the evening — find shade, hydrate, take breaks
Settle into your fireworks spot; bring a blanket or low chairs
Event wraps up. Depart via the appropriate gate

fort_meade.key_alert: Early July evenings have been running hot and humid, with highs near 100°F. Plan for shade breaks, sunscreen, and extra water.

dc_july4.key_action: REGISTER FOR FREE FIREWORKS TICKETS NOW
dc_july4.clear_bag_policy: Only one small clear bag or small clutch per person

transportation_options.reason: Metrorail runs until 2 AM with free fares after 5 PM
Last train at 10:55 PM (before 11 PM fireworks)
Street closures June 24–July 11; garages jammed for hours

dc_july4.recommended_plan: Drive to Greenbelt Metro, free ride home after 5 PM

itinerary.activity: Leave Fort Meade, drive to Greenbelt Metro
Board Metro Green Line toward L'Enfant Plaza / Smithsonian
Arrive National Mall. Great American State Fair opens (free)
Morning program: Salute to America Parade + state-fair pavilions
Early lunch before security lines build
Pass through security (clear bag, ID, no chairs/coolers/aerosols)
Hottest part of day — air-conditioned Smithsonian museums (free heat break)
Exit with crowd toward Metro station (30–60 min to clear)
Free Metro ride back to Greenbelt, drive home

key_alerts: Metro only viable return option after midnight
